title: The Icebreaker
description: Your first talk. Introduce yourself and your AI journey. How did you get here? What tools changed things for you? What are you building now?
prompt_template: I was [emotion] when Claude Code [thing that happened]. My name is [name] and I [what you do]. Here's how AI changed my workflow...
tips: Keep it personal. This isn't a tech talk, it's a story.
One specific moment is better than a timeline of your whole career.
End with what you're building now or what you're excited about.

title: The Workflow Walkthrough
description: Walk the room through how you actually build something. From idea to shipped. Show the real process, not the polished version.
prompt_template: I was [emotion] when Claude Code [thing that happened]. Let me show you how I [specific workflow]...
tips: Pick ONE workflow, not your entire setup.
Show the messy parts. The retries, the wrong turns.
If you demo, have a backup plan if tech fails. The story is what matters.
End with the outcome - what shipped, what improved, what you learned.

title: The Horror Story
description: Tell us about the time AI went wrong. The deleted database. The wrong API call. The PR that broke prod. Bonus points if you show how you recovered.
prompt_template: I was [emotion] when Claude Code [catastrophic thing]. Here's what happened and what I learned...
tips: Be honest about what went wrong and why.
The audience wants to learn from your mistake, not judge you.
End with the lesson. What do you do differently now?
These are usually the most memorable talks. Lean into the drama.

title: The Before/After
description: Compare how you did something before AI tools and how you do it now. Could be a specific task, a project, or your entire development process.
prompt_template: I was [emotion] when Claude Code [thing that happened]. Here's what [task] looked like before, and what it looks like now...
tips: Be specific. 'I used to spend 3 hours on X, now it takes 20 minutes' beats 'AI made me faster.'
Show both sides honestly. What got better AND what got worse or stayed the same.
Numbers help. Time saved, lines of code, bugs caught, projects shipped.

title: The Tool Review
description: You tried a tool, framework, MCP server, or workflow for a real project. Tell us what worked, what didn't, and whether you'd recommend it.
prompt_template: I was [emotion] when Claude Code [thing that happened]. I've been using [tool] for [duration] and here's my honest take...
tips: Real usage only. Don't review something you tried for 10 minutes.
Compare it to what you used before. What made you switch?
Give a clear verdict: would you recommend it, and for what kind of builder?

title: The Live Build
description: Build something from scratch in 5 minutes using AI tools. High risk, high reward. The audience watches you prompt, iterate, and ship in real time.
prompt_template: I was [emotion] when Claude Code [thing that happened]. Watch me build [thing] live right now...
tips: Pick something small and achievable in 5 minutes. A CLI tool, a component, a script.
Narrate what you're doing and why. Don't just type in silence.
If it breaks, that's content. Talk through the debugging.
Have a pre-built version as backup so you can show the end result either way.

title: The Setup Tour
description: Walk us through your AI development environment. CLAUDE.md, MCP servers, hooks, skills, editor config. The full harness.
prompt_template: I was [emotion] when Claude Code [thing that happened]. Let me show you how I've set up my environment...
tips: Focus on the WHY behind your choices, not just the WHAT.
Pick 2-3 most impactful parts of your setup, don't try to cover everything.
Share what you'd change if starting over.

title: The Discovery
description: Share something you just learned. A technique, a prompt pattern, a hidden feature, a tweet thread that changed how you work. Fresh and timely.
prompt_template: I was [emotion] when Claude Code [thing that happened]. I just discovered [thing] and it changed everything...
tips: Recency matters. This should be something from the last week or two.
Link to the source if it came from Twitter/blog/video.
Show a quick before/after of how you applied it.

title: Challenge Accepted
description: The audience gives you a challenge. Build it, fix it, or explain it in 5 minutes. Pure improvisation with AI tools.
prompt_template: The audience challenged me to [challenge]. Let's see what happens...
tips: This is for experienced speakers comfortable with uncertainty.
Narrate your thinking process out loud.
It's okay to fail. The audience is rooting for you.
Have the host pre-screen challenges to keep them achievable.
